{{Short description="Fundamental force binding quarks into protons, neutrons, and other hadrons}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Strong interaction
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Fundamental interaction]], [[Quark]], [[Gluon]], [[Atomic nucleus]], [[Quantum chromodynamics]]
| mediated_by = [[Gluon]]s
| range = ~10⁻¹⁵ m
| strength = 1 (reference, strongest force)
}}

The '''strong interaction''' (or '''strong nuclear force''') is the strongest of the four [[fundamental interaction]]s of nature. It is the force that holds [[quark]]s together to form [[proton]]s, [[neutron]]s, and other [[hadron]]s, and also binds protons and neutrons to form [[atomic nucleus|atomic nuclei]] (the [[nuclear force]]). The strong interaction is described by the theory of [[quantum chromodynamics]] (QCD).

== Properties ==
* Mediated by massless [[gluon]]s
* Acts on particles with [[colour charge]] (quarks and gluons)
* Extremely short range: ~10⁻¹⁵ m (1 femtometre)
* Strength: approximately 137 times the [[electromagnetic force]], 10⁶ times the [[weak interaction]], 10³⁸ times [[gravity]]
* Exhibits [[colour confinement]] — quarks and gluons cannot exist in isolation

== Quantum chromodynamics ==
{{Main|Quantum chromodynamics}}
QCD is a [[non-abelian gauge theory]] with [[SU(3)]] symmetry. The Lagrangian is:

:<math>\mathcal{L}_{\text{QCD}} = \bar{\psi}_i(i\gamma^\mu (D_\mu)_{ij} - m\delta_{ij})\psi_j - \frac{1}{4}G^a_{\mu\nu}G^{\mu\nu}_a</math>

where <math>G^a_{\mu\nu}</math> is the gluon field strength tensor.

=== Key features ===
* '''Colour confinement''' — colour-charged particles cannot be isolated
* '''Asymptotic freedom''' — at high energies/short distances, quarks behave as free particles
* '''Eight gluons''' — corresponding to the generators of SU(3)

== Nuclear force ==
{{Main|Nuclear force}}
The residual strong interaction (analogous to [[van der Waals force]]s between neutral atoms) binds nucleons in nuclei. It is mediated by [[meson]]s (primarily [[pion]]s) rather than gluons.

[[Yukawa potential]]:
:<math>V(r) = -g^2\frac{e^{-r/r_0}}{r}</math>
where <math>r_0 \approx 1.4\ \text{fm}</math> is the range.

== Role in nature ==
* Holds [[atomic nucleus|nuclei]] together against [[Coulomb's law|electrostatic repulsion]]
* Responsible for ~99% of the mass of [[proton]]s and [[neutron]]s (via [[mass–energy equivalence]] of QCD binding energy)
* Governs [[hadron]] production in [[particle accelerator]]s
* Forms [[quark–gluon plasma]] at extreme temperatures (early universe, heavy-ion collisions)

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Fundamental interaction]]
* [[Gluon]]
* [[Hadron]]
* [[Neutron]]
* [[Nuclear physics]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quantum chromodynamics]]
* [[Quark]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Strong interaction| ]]
[[Category:Fundamental interactions]]
[[Category:Nuclear physics]]
[[Category:Quantum chromodynamics]]
